user_message: Can you help me assess the risk? The customer (Lucerne Publishing) is asking about migration architecture.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 10:01 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 10:56 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 15:37 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Lucerne Publishing migration architecture

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-A trap (Mismatches tone (formal where casual was established)); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-A failure mode (Mismatches tone (formal where casual was established))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-A -- Mismatches tone (formal where casual was established)
